title: Dedicated capacity deployment readiness & SLA targets
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Chen <laura@novaml.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Recap: Dedicated capacity discovery — next steps & risks

Laura — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment before the technical deep-dive: 

What we heard (pain & goals)
- Primary goal: move latency-sensitive inference for NovaML in-house (private VPC) with predictable cost.
- Pain: variability on shared endpoints, regulatory requirement to control keys / egress for a subset of data, and bursty training-style jobs that compete for capacity.
- Target success metrics: initial SLA target = 99.9% uptime for serving, p95 serving latency <200ms for requests <512 tokens, and predictable per-hour capacity for batch jobs.

Draft sizing & isolation assumptions (starting point)
- Initial reserved pool: 8x A100 or equivalent (mixed T4s for cheaper batch lanes) — we modeled 4k concurrent short inferences (~<1k token) with room to burst to on-demand.
- Isolation: dedicated tenant pool in your VPC (no shared host access) + customer-managed KMS for keys.
- Scaling plan: reserved baseline + autoscale burst to our shared capacity with policy-based fallback (priority routing for critical routes).

Risks / open questions
- Procurement timeline and whether legal will accept our standard DPA / SOC2 evidence — we can provide SOC2 report and network diagrams (attached).
- Confirmation of network egress restrictions (are outbound IPs acceptable or do you need egress blocked entirely?).
- Exact traffic profile: token length distribution and QPS spikes (can you share a 2-week sample?).

Next steps
1) Confirm the isolation & SLA targets above or call out deviations by EOD Thu (Aug 27).
2) Schedule a technical deep-dive with Evelyn (solutions engineer) to lock down sizing and network setup (30–45min).
3) We’ll circulate a SOW draft (attached) and a high-level timeline for a pilot (2–4 weeks).

Attachments: nova_dedicated_capacity_SOW-draft.pdf, gcp-network-design-diagram.png

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192



From: Laura Chen <laura@novaml.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: NovaML Labs Procurement <procurement@novaml.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 26 Aug 2026 09:34:00 -0700

- Procurement: we prefer a monthly committed billing cadence for the reserved baseline, with a pre-authorized burst buffer to handle spikes. Can you provide a sample monthly invoice layout?
- Traffic: we can share anonymized telemetry for the last 30 days by EOD Thu; it will show heavy morning spikes and a long tail of batch jobs overnight.

One ask: can your solution support an air-gapped pilot or at least an on-prem-like environment? Our compliance team would like to see that option scoped.

We’re good to set up the deep-dive — Evelyn, can you propose times?

title: Redlines: Exhibit D (Pricing Schedule) — fee lift mechanism clarification
preview:
From: Elena Price <elena.price@acmehealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.inference>
Cc: procurement@acmehealth.com
Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Redlines: Exhibit D - Pricing Schedule and Fee Lift Mechanism

Chloe —

Attached is Acme's redline to Exhibit D (pricing schedule). The primary open items are:

1) Fee lift mechanism (Section 2(b)): Acme's finance team pushed back on a single, uncapped percentage step when monthly committed usage is exceeded. They're asking for either a gradual step-up (tiered) or an annual smoothing credit so spikes don't create a retrospective lift for one month that skews the whole year.

2) Caps & floors: We propose preserving the floor (minimum monthly fee) but ask for a hard annual cap on aggregate uplift so budget doesn't blow out.

3) Packaging mapping: The 'SKU mapping' note in the order schedule is ambiguous — does fee lift apply at the SKU level, product family, or consolidated account? We need explicit language for how usage is aggregated.

Please confirm receipt and whether Redwood can accept (i) tiered lift thresholds, (ii) an annual cap, and (iii) clarified SKU aggregation. We prefer redline edits but have also attached a short rationale doc.

Regards,
Elena Price
Head of Platform Procurement
Acme Health

[Attachment: Exhibit D - Pricing Schedule (Acme redline).pdf]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.inference>
To: Elena Price <elena.price@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redlines: Exhibit D - Pricing Schedule and Fee Lift Mechanism

Elena —

Thanks, received and read. Quick notes while I pull the team together:

• We hear the concern on short-term spikes causing disproportionate uplift.
• We're open to a tiered approach but need to preserve the ability to protect against sustained over-usage that materially impacts capacity/costs.
• SKU aggregation: Redwood's standard is per "billing identity" (account + order schedule) but we can define product-family grouping if that better fits your internal chargebacks.

I've attached a short alternative language (Proposed language v1.docx) that (a) introduces a 3-tier lift (5%/12%/25%), (b) includes an annual smoothing credit if monthly lift exceeds X% of annual spend, and (c) ties aggregation to the OrderSchedule ID unless parties agree in writing to a different grouping.

Would you be available for a 30‑minute call Wednesday to walk through? If so we'll invite pricing + legal.

Best,
Chloe Martin
Senior Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: Proposed language v1.docx]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.inference>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 13:55:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Acme redline — internal pricing/legal thoughts

Chloe / team —

Read through Acme's redline and Chloe's proposed language. High-level concerns from legal/pricing:

• Annual cap: reasonable to offer a hard cap on "aggregate annual uplift" but we need to define carve-outs (force majeure, negotiated temporary bursts, billing errors). Suggest: "Aggregate annual uplift shall not exceed 15% of the Annual Recurring Fee, excluding amounts attributable to documented system misuse or emergency bursts pre-approved by Redwood."

• Tiered lift: prefer tiered, but cap the maximum step so we have a predictable worst-case (e.g., maximum uplift of 25% in a single month and a maximum across year capped as above).

• SKU aggregation: require we define "Billing Unit" in the Order Schedule. Default = consolidated account, opt-out by mutual agreement. This prevents Acme from fragmenting consumption to avoid lift.

Next steps I propose:
1) Agree internal positions (Chloe/Ben/Logan) and send a consolidated counter with tracked redline.
2) Offer Acme the option for an "annual smoothing credit" (applied to the last quarter) if monthly lifts materially exceed expectation.

Marking up Chloe's doc and will send language suggestions by EOD.

-Marissa

Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 09:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Acme redline — packaging / SKU mapping impact

Team —

Some practical examples to think about for the SKU aggregation question (these are hypothetical):

• Example A — Single product family: Acme runs "CareAssist" across 3 hospitals under one OrderSchedule. If aggregation is per OrderSchedule, the lift triggers when total CareAssist tokens exceed thresholds across hospitals (preferred for Redwood).

• Example B — Fragmented SKUs: If Acme defines separate SKUs per hospital, they could push usage to different lines to avoid per-line thresholds; we protect against this by making the default aggregation consolidated by Billing Identity.

Proposal: default = consolidated account (OrderSchedule ID). If Acme requests different grouping, require written approval + define a migration window (60 days) to reconcile historic usage. Also recommend a minimum reporting granularity clause (monthly token rolls) so both sides can see the behavior that caused lift.

Attaching a short CSV stub we can send to Acme showing the token aggregation by OrderSchedule for a sample month.

-Ben

[Attachment: OrderSchedule-attachment.csv]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.inference>
To: Elena Price <elena.price@acmehealth.com>
Cc: procurement@acmehealth.com, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference>
Date: Wed, 26 Aug 2026 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redlines: Exhibit D - Pricing Schedule and Fee Lift Mechanism (Consolidated counter)

Elena —

Appreciate the clear redline. Attached is Redwood's consolidated counter (Exhibit D - Proposed Redline.docx). Key points: 

1) Tiered lift: 3 tiers at 5% / 12% / 20% applied to the monthly invoice when the usage in a billing month exceeds the committed monthly token threshold by >10%, >30%, and >60% respectively.

2) Annual uplift cap: aggregate uplift in any 12‑month period shall not exceed 15% of the Annual Recurring Fee, excluding (a) documented misuse, (b) emergency bursts pre-approved by Customer, and (c) amounts resulting from post-billing corrections.

3) Aggregation: default Billing Unit = OrderSchedule ID (consolidated by default). Any alternative grouping must be mutually agreed in writing and subject to a 60-day reconciliation/transition period.

4) Annual smoothing credit: if total monthly lifts across the year exceed the annual cap threshold, Customer is eligible for a smoothing credit applied to the final invoice of the 12‑month period (details in paragraph 4.3).

We also included a clean example table in the counter showing a sample invoice calculation for a spike month vs. smoothed year. Please review the counter and let us know whether procurement + finance can accept these guardrails or prefer alternate percentages. If helpful, I'll set up a 30-minute call (propose Thu 9am PT).

DocuSign/next steps: we've prepared a DocuSign envelope (link below) for the updated Exhibit D if Acme is ready to accept. Otherwise we can continue markups.

Thanks,
Chloe Martin

Redwood Inference

> • We're open to a tiered approach but need to preserve the ability to protect against sustained over-usage that materially impacts capacity/costs.
>
> [Attachment: Proposed language v1.docx]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.inference>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 09:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Acme redline — packaging / SKU mapping impact

Team —

Some practical examples to think about for the SKU aggregation question (these are hypothetical):

• Example A — Single product family: Acme runs "CareAssist" across 3 hospitals under one OrderSchedule. If aggregation is per OrderSchedule, the lift triggers when total CareAssist tokens exceed thresholds across hospitals (preferred for Redwood).

• Example B — Fragmented SKUs: If Acme defines separate SKUs per hospital, they could push usage to different lines to avoid per-line thresholds; we protect against this by making the default aggregation consolidated by Billing Identity.

Proposal: default = consolidated account (OrderSchedule ID). If Acme requests different grouping, require written approval + define a migration window (60 days) to reconcile historic usage. Also recommend a minimum reporting granularity clause (monthly token rolls) so both sides can see the behavior that caused lift.

Attaching a short CSV stub we can send to Acme showing the token aggregation by OrderSchedule for a sample month.

-Ben

[Attachment: OrderSchedule-attachment.csv]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.inference>
To: Elena Price <elena.price@acmehealth.com>
Cc: procurement@acmehealth.com, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference>
Date: Wed, 26 Aug 2026 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redlines: Exhibit D - Pricing Schedule and Fee Lift Mechanism (Consolidated counter)

Elena —

Appreciate the clear redline. Attached is Redwood's consolidated counter (Exhibit D - Proposed Redline.docx). Key points: 

1) Tiered lift: 3 tiers at 5% / 12% / 20% applied to the monthly invoice when the usage in a billing month exceeds the committed monthly token threshold by >10%, >30%, and >60% respectively.

2) Annual uplift cap: aggregate uplift in any 12‑month period shall not exceed 15% of the Annual Recurring Fee, excluding (a) documented misuse, (b) emergency bursts pre-approved by Customer, and (c) amounts resulting from post-billing corrections.

3) Aggregation: default Billing Unit = OrderSchedule ID (consolidated by default). Any alternative grouping must be mutually agreed in writing and subject to a 60-day reconciliation/transition period.

4) Annual smoothing credit: if total monthly lifts across the year exceed the annual cap threshold, Customer is eligible for a smoothing credit applied to the final invoice of the 12‑month period (details in paragraph 4.3).

We also included a clean example table in the counter showing a sample invoice calculation for a spike month vs. smoothed year. Please review the counter and let us know whether procurement + finance can accept these guardrails or prefer alternate percentages. If helpful, I'll set up a 30-minute call (propose Thu 9am PT).

DocuSign/next steps: we've prepared a DocuSign envelope (link below) for the updated Exhibit D if Acme is ready to accept. Otherwise we can continue markups.

Thanks,
Chloe Martin
Senior Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: Exhibit D - Proposed Redline.docx]
[Attachment: OrderSchedule-attachment.csv]

DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/placeholder/ACME-REDW-0824

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the consolidation. Procurement and finance will review the proposed tiering and annual cap tomorrow and aim to revert with either (a) acceptance of the counter, (b) small percentage tweaks, or (c) a request for a brief clarifying call.

title: Purchase acknowledgement timing & payment rail concessions
preview:
From: Luke Harris <lharris@mediwave.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
Cc: MediWave Procurement <procurement@mediwave.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Aug 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Purchase acknowledgement timing + payment rails for Redwood engagement

Hi Alex / Vanessa,

Thanks again for the demo last week — team liked the latency profile and the routing options. Procurement here needs to lock down a few operational details before we can issue the blanket PO:

- PO acceptance window: our AR ops requires a 30-day acceptance window from issuance. Can Redwood accept that as the official PO acknowledgement window?
- Invoicing cadence: we'd prefer one consolidated monthly invoice (all usage for the month) rather than multiple per-deployment statements. Is Redwood able to provide a consolidated monthly invoice by BU?
- PO fields: our ERP requires the PO to include a specific set of headers (bill_to_entity, ship_to, cost_center, project_code). We will supply a PO template — can Redwood map those fields to the invoice lines?
- Net terms: Mediwave standard is net45 for SaaS vendors. Can you confirm Redwood will provide net45 for this contract?
- Discount: we'll evaluate a quarterly commitment (3 months minimum) for a usage band. Is there a volume discount or prepay concession you can offer if we commit?

I attached our PO template for reference (pdf). Would appreciate a short note with firm answers so we can proceed to issuing the PO.

Best,
Luke
Luke Harris
Procurement Manager, MediWave Health
Attachment: MediWave_PO_template.pdf (pdf)


From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Luke Harris <lharris@mediwave.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Aug 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Purchase acknowledgement timing + payment rails for Redwood engagement

Hi Luke — thanks for the clear list. Quick answers below and a couple of asks so we align the invoice mapping: 

- PO acceptance window: We can accept a 30-day acknowledgement window for the initial PO. For larger volume or multi-entity POs we prefer a 10-business-day operational acceptance, but we're able to make an exception for Mediwave and accept 30 days for this agreement.

- Invoicing cadence: We support consolidated monthly invoices by billing entity. We can split by BU/cost_center if you include those fields on the PO.

- PO fields: We can map bill_to_entity, cost_center and project_code to invoice metadata. Please confirm the exact field names in the ERP export (your template helps).

- Net terms: Redwood standard is net30, but for enterprise procurement we can move to net45 on committed deals. We'll confirm with Finance and follow up (cc'ing Ben).

- Discount: We typically offer a quarterly-commitment discount and a deeper annual prepay. I'll propose a baseline: 5% discount for a quarterly committed volume band, 8% for annual prepay. I'll have Finance confirm.

I've attached a sample invoice and a Redwood PO-to-invoice mapping doc for your ops team to review. If that looks good, I can draft a short amendment to the SOW to lock in net45 and the discounted band.

Next steps: Luke — if you can return the filled PO template and confirm billing entity labels, I'll loop in Legal/Finance to prepare the amendment.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
M: (415) 555-0142

Subject: FW: Purchase acknowledgement timing + payment rails for Redwood engagement

Ben / Vanessa — forwarding Luke's ask. TL;DR: Mediwave wants 30-day PO acceptance, consolidated monthly invoice split by BU, net45, and a commitment discount (quarterly or annual).

Questions for Finance:
- Can we extend net45 to this enterprise deal? Any thresholds (ARR/commit) we need to hit?
- Does consolidated monthly invoicing with custom invoice-level metadata (cost_center/project_code) create any AR friction?
- Discount guidance: propose 5% for quarterly commit, 8% for annual prepay. Runway?

They've sent their PO template — attached above. I'm recommending we accept 30-day PO acknowledgement as a one-off since ops will like the longer window. Want to avoid giving net60.

Appreciate quick sync so I can close this out for the week.

—Alex

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FW: Purchase acknowledgement timing + payment rails for Redwood engagement

Alex — finance has reviewed. Short answers: 

- Net terms: We can offer net45 for enterprise deals >= $150k ARR or equivalent annual spend. For this specific Mediwave trajectory (you noted expected monthly usage ~ $18k), we can approve net45 for the first 12 months with the SOW amendment and a standard credit check.

- Invoicing: Consolidated monthly invoices by billing entity are fine. We can include ERP fields (bill_to_entity, cost_center, project_code) as invoice metadata. Ops note: please ensure PO_number maps to our invoice reference so their ERP can auto-match.

- Discounts: Approve the proposed bands, but add guardrails: 5% for a rolling quarterly committed band (3-month minimum with a minimum monthly spend floor), 8% for annual prepay or annual committed minimum. Discounts apply to consumption charges only, not professional services.

- PO acceptance window: 30 days is acceptable as a one-time concession; our operational SLA prefers 10 business days, but we'll take 30 for issuance.

I'll prepare a short Finance approval note to attach to the amendment. CC me on the amendment draft.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Finance Lead, Redwood Inference

Ben / Vanessa — forwarding Luke's ask. TL;DR: Mediwave wants 30-day PO acceptance, consolidated monthly invoice split by BU, net45, and a commitment discount (quarterly or annual).

Questions for Finance:
- Can we extend net45 to this enterprise deal? Any thresholds (ARR/commit) we need to hit?
- Does consolidated monthly invoicing with custom invoice-level metadata (cost_center/project_code) create any AR friction?
- Discount guidance: propose 5% for quarterly commit, 8% for annual prepay. Runway?

They've sent their PO template — attached above. I'm recommending we accept 30-day PO acknowledgement as a one-off since ops will like the longer window. Want to avoid giving net60.

Appreciate quick sync so I can close this out for the week.

—Alex

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FW: Purchase acknowledgement timing + payment rails for Redwood engagement

Alex — finance has reviewed. Short answers: 

- Net terms: We can offer net45 for enterprise deals >= $150k ARR or equivalent annual spend. For this specific Mediwave trajectory (you noted expected monthly usage ~ $18k), we can approve net45 for the first 12 months with the SOW amendment and a standard credit check.

- Invoicing: Consolidated monthly invoices by billing entity are fine. We can include ERP fields (bill_to_entity, cost_center, project_code) as invoice metadata. Ops note: please ensure PO_number maps to our invoice reference so their ERP can auto-match.

- Discounts: Approve the proposed bands, but add guardrails: 5% for a rolling quarterly committed band (3-month minimum with a minimum monthly spend floor), 8% for annual prepay or annual committed minimum. Discounts apply to consumption charges only, not professional services.

- PO acceptance window: 30 days is acceptable as a one-time concession; our operational SLA prefers 10 business days, but we'll take 30 for issuance.

I'll prepare a short Finance approval note to attach to the amendment. CC me on the amendment draft.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Finance Lead, Redwood Inference

1) PO acceptance window: Redwood will accept the 30-day PO acknowledgement window for this engagement as a one-time operational concession. For future renewals we will default to a 10-business-day acceptance.

2) Invoicing cadence: We will provide a consolidated monthly invoice for all usage tied to the billing entity. We can include your ERP fields (bill_to_entity, cost_center, project_code) as invoice metadata and ensure PO_number maps to our invoice reference for auto-match.

3) Net terms: Finance has approved net45 for the first 12 months of this engagement, subject to standard credit checks and the signed amendment.

4) Discount: We can offer: 5% discount for a quarterly committed usage band (3-month minimum with a floor of $10k/month), or 8% for an annual prepay/committed minimum. Discounts apply to consumption charges only (not professional services or third-party pass-throughs).

5) Next steps: If this alignment looks good, please:

   - I'll attach the Finance approval note and send a one-page amendment (DocSend) that captures net45 and the discount band.

I've attached Redwood_invoice_sample.xlsx and our mapping guidance. DocSend link for the amendment: https://docsend.com/view/example-amendment.

Thanks — happy to jump on a quick call if you want to run through the mapping.

Best,

title: Recap + Mutual Action Plan (MAP) — Kalypso pilot and procurement timeline
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Avery Chen <avery@kalypso.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 22 Aug 2026 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Recap + Mutual Action Plan (MAP) — Kalypso pilot and procurement timeline

Hi Avery (and team),

Thanks again for the great discussion today. Quick recap from our side and a proposed MAP so everyone has clarity on milestones, owners and dates. I noted the items you called out on the call and added what we need to move to an SOW and pilot start.

Summary of pain and goals (as stated on the call):
- Main pain: inconsistent NLU baseline across customer support channels; current latency spikes are causing timeouts for higher-volume threads.
- Business goal: 40% reduction in average handling time and sub-200ms inference for FAQ reranking in the proof-of-concept.
- Success criteria: latency < 200ms p95 on 4k tokens/session, >= 0.75 NLU intent accuracy lift on test set, end-customer pilot enabled on two live channels.

Proposed Mutual Action Plan (draft) — owners & target dates:
1) Security & Compliance review — Kalypso: Legal & Procurement to confirm SOC2 scope; Redwood: provide SOC2 report + security appendix.
   - Owner: Jordan Lee (Kalypso legal) / Ben Carter (Redwood)
   - Target: 2026-09-03

2) Pilot technical kickoff & data prep (sample dataset handoff, sanitize, schema)
   - Owner: Kalypso Data Team / Vivek Kulkarni (Redwood SE)
   - Target: 2026-09-10

3) SOW draft & pricing confirmation (inc. optional Dedicated capacity)
   - Owner: Redwood Sales (Ben) / Kalypso Procurement
   - Target: Draft by 2026-09-07, finalize by 2026-09-17

4) Pilot run (2-week baseline + 2-week tuning)
   - Owner: Kalypso + Redwood CS (Monica Patel)
   - Target: Start 2026-09-20, complete 2026-10-04

5) Exec review & decision (expand / purchase commitment)
   - Owner: Kalypso Execs / Ben Carter
   - Target: 2026-10-12

Open items / asks from Redwood:
- Kalypso procurement target for PO/signature (we noted a possible 4-week procurement window).
- Sample dataset (anonymized) and expected throughput profile (avg QPS, peak QPS).
- Any legal/contract redlines to route to our legal team (please copy Jordan).

Attachments: kalypso_MAP_v1.xlsx (MAP draft with rows for milestones, stakeholders, and acceptance criteria)

Next steps (clear actions):
- Ben: circulate MAP v1 (attached) — DONE (this email).
- Kalypso: confirm procurement timeline and share sample data by 2026-08-27.
- Vivek: schedule a 60-min kickoff to scope infra and data needs (can you propose times?).

I propose a 30-min check-in next Thurs 2026-08-27 to lock dates — does that work for you?

Thanks — will follow up with the MAP v1 in Drive.
—
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Avery Chen <avery@kalypso.ai>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 11:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap + Mutual Action Plan (MAP) — Kalypso pilot and procurement timeline

Folks — quick technical notes following the call and MAP. I want to call out a couple of risk items and propose mitigations so they can be reflected in the MAP/SOW: 
- Latency target: achieving p95 <200ms depends on the model variant and token window. For live channel routing we should plan for A/B testing between a 4-bit quantized variant (lower cost) and a 16-bit variant for peak hours.
- KV cache warmup: first-token latency may spike for cold starts; recommend we include a warmup plan (small synthetic pre-warm job) before the pilot load test.
- Data format: the JSON schema you mentioned is fine; please include an example of a multi-turn transcript (we need to validate tokenization).

Action items for Redwood (technical):
- Vivek to supply pilot_requirements.md (attached) with sample request payloads and expected QPS targets.
- Vivek/Monica to join Kalypso data handoff call (propose times on 2026-08-28).

title: Post-call recap & tech follow-ups: reranker integration and eval plan
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Chen <oliver@searchlytics.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-21T15:10:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call recap & tech follow-ups: reranker integration and eval plan

Hi Oliver — thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment: 

Summary of what we covered:
- SearchLytics wants to add a reranker layer to the existing retrieval pipeline to improve relevance for top results (product search + FAQs).
- Primary goals: increase CTR on top-3 results, reduce incorrect high-ranked items (precision at top-k), and maintain sub-100ms p99 latency for the endpoint.
- Constraints: existing retrieval uses an embedding-based ANN; budget target is ~30% increase in inference cost for a 10-15% lift in top-k relevance.

Action items we proposed (next 2 weeks):
1) Tech qualification: SDK/auth and routing — what endpoints will call Redwood (edge vs internal service)? Which auth model for the pilot (API keys vs short-lived tokens)?
2) Eval plan: provide 1k labelled queries (you suggested product + FAQ slices) and baseline logs for current ranking metrics. We’ll propose an eval rubric (NDCG@3, MRR, precision@1/3) and top_k candidates to test.
3) Integration checklist: example SDK init, preferred response shape (structured output / tool-calling usage), and a plan for caching/kv prefix reuse.

Specific asks from you:
- Confirm which service(s) will call Redwood (public-facing search-api vs internal ranking-service).
- Share sample queries + a small CSV of ~200 labelled ground-truth examples for an initial smoke eval.
- Any firm constraints on model families (open models only) or quantization tolerance?

I’m looping in Marissa (solutions eng) and Karthik (integrations) to handle the technical questions below — Marissa/Karthik, can you weigh in on the routing/auth options and proposed eval metrics?

Attachments: I’m attaching a short primer on reranker approaches we reference in the pilot (rerank_primer.pdf) and a strawman architecture diagram (searchlytics_arch_diagram_v1.png).

Proposed next meeting: 30-minute technical workshop — are you available Wed 2026-08-26 11:00 PT?

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123
redwood.com


From: Oliver Chen <oliver@searchlytics.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-22T09:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & tech follow-ups: reranker integration and eval plan

Thanks Ben — helpful recap. A few clarifications from our side: 

1) Callers: the traffic will originate from our ranking-service (internal microservice) which sits behind our public search-api. For the pilot we prefer calls to Redwood from ranking-service (private network egress).
2) Auth: we prefer short-lived mTLS tokens if possible (we can support ephemeral cert rotation). Is that supported on Redwood Private / Dedicated?
3) Eval data: I can put together 250 labelled queries (mix of product search and FAQ) by EOD Friday. We can include session context for ~30% of queries.
4) Latency: p50 target is 30ms, p95 under 80ms; p99 < 200ms is acceptable for reranker-only calls (we will still enforce an overall end-to-end budget).

Question for the engineer: when you talk about top_k tuning, do you expect we run the retriever for k=50 and then rerank to return top 5? Or do you recommend a smaller candidate set (k=10) and more expensive reranker? How do you measure tradeoffs during eval?

Happy to run the workshop Wed 8/26 11:00 PT.

- Oliver
SearchLytics — Head of Relevance

> On 2026-08-22 09:40, Oliver Chen <oliver@searchlytics.ai> wrote:
> > Thanks Ben — helpful recap. A few clarifications from our side: 
> > ...


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Oliver Chen <oliver@searchlytics.ai>
Date: 2026-08-24T11:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & tech follow-ups: reranker integration and eval plan

Hi all — quick technical answers and a proposed sweep plan. Long-winded but wanted to be concrete so the workshop is productive.

Auth & networking
- Redwood Dedicated and Redwood Private support short-lived token exchange via our identity broker and also mTLS for on-prem/private links. For your ranking-service pattern, we recommend the following:
  - Issue short-lived JWTs via your identity provider -> Redwood token exchange (1 minute TTL for pilot) OR
  - mTLS for Private endpoint if you want no outward internet egress and cert-based auth (we can provide instructions for cert pinning).
- Notes: mTLS option requires network allowlists and an ingress cert; we'll share a Terraform snippet during the workshop.

Response shape & structured output
- We recommend a structured JSON response where the reranker returns: [candidate_id, score, normalized_score, explanation_spans, model_version]. Example: 
  {
    "id": "doc_123",
    "score": 8.27,
    "norm_score": 0.94,
    "explanation": ["query_terms_in_title", "semantic_match_title_body"],
    "version": "rerank-v1-quant"
  }
- If you want token-level highlights for UI, use our optional spans field (byte offsets) — small size impact.
- For tool-calling / structured responses: we can return a secondary 'actions' array if you want the reranker to trigger downstream enrichment (e.g., fetch price, availability). That fits our structured output spec.

Candidate set sizing & eval recommendation
- Tradeoffs: larger candidate sets (k=50) give better oracle upper-bound for relevance but cost more and increase response assembly time. Smaller sets (k=10) reduce cost but can miss good items if retriever recall is weak.
- Practical pilot configuration (we suggest running both in parallel against your 250/1k labelled queries):
  1) Latency-optimized: retriever_k=20 -> reranker on top 20 -> return top 5. Aim: p95 reranker < 80ms.
  2) Quality-optimized: retriever_k=50 -> reranker on top 50 -> return top 5. Aim: measure NDCG@3 and MRR gains.
  3) Optional: retriever_k=10 + heavier reranker (larger model) for mobile/offline budgets.

Eval metrics & logging
- We suggest NDCG@1/3, MRR, precision@1 and precision@3 as primary metrics. For statistical significance, run bootstrap resampling across queries.
- Instrument per-query latency and token cost; include model_version in logs so regresses are traceable.
- Baseline: use your current retrieval-only ranked list as the baseline; we will compute relative lift.

Small sample SDK snippets (Python) — this is a minimal example to call the ranking API and parse structured output:

import requests

url = "https://api.redwood-inference.com/v1/rerank"
headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <SHORT_LIVED_TOKEN>", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
payload = {
  "query": "wireless earbuds battery life",
  "candidates": ["doc_12", "doc_34", "doc_9"],
  "return_spans": True
}
resp = requests.post(url, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=1.0)
results = resp.json().get("results", [])
# results -> list of {id, score, normalized_score, explanation, version}

Attachments: rerank_primer.pdf contains evaluation primer + example pipelines. I attached a strawman architecture (searchlytics_arch_diagram_v1.png) with caller placement and network flow.

Proposed workshop agenda (30 min):
- 5m: confirm auth/network pattern and which endpoint will call (ranking-service)
- 10m: review eval plan and candidate sweep config
- 10m: walk through SDK example and integration checklist (response shape, caching, error handling)
- 5m: agree on next steps & timeline

I’ll come prepared with Terraform snippets for mTLS and a Jupyter notebook for the NDCG/MRR compute.

—Marissa
Marissa Cole
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-26T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & tech follow-ups: reranker integration and eval plan

Marissa — this is great, thanks for the detail. Quick confirmation:

- We will call the Redwood Dedicated endpoint from ranking-service over our private peering; we prefer the mTLS flow for pilot. Please send the Terraform snippet and cert steps in the workshop.
- We’ll prepare the 250 labelled queries today and share the CSV + a sample of session-context rows.
- For the candidate sweep we’ll run the retriever_k in [20, 50] as you suggested. We’ll also include a small heavy-reranker run with k=10 for mobile tests.

I’m good for Wed 8/26 11:00 PT — see you on the call. Can you add a calendar invite and include the Terraform link and the Jupyter notebook link ahead of time?

Thanks — Oliver

title: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - escrowed capacity proposal
preview:
From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@brightcart.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-24T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - request

Camila —

We have a hard launch scheduled for 2026-09-01 and our traffic projections show a concentrated spike during the first 10 days (mainly U.S. traffic). We expect baseline traffic of ~800k tokens/day, rising to a peak of ~3M tokens/day for ~7–10 days during promos.

Two asks:
1) Can Redwood provide a short-term uplift (rate-limit / quota increase) to cover that window? We need the capacity change to be in place by 2026-08-31 18:00 PDT.
2) What would the commercial terms look like for temporary overrun protections (prorated true-up, any launch credits, and rollback guarantees)?

We want to avoid a hard product disruption mid-launch. BrightCart is evaluating a dedicated contract but this is the immediate risk. If you can propose a quick path (pricing + ops steps) by EOD tomorrow that would help our stakeholders sign off.

Jordan
Jordan Lee
Launch Ops — BrightCart
launchops@brightcart.com
(415) 555-0198

---
Attachment: traffic_projection_q3.csv (csv)


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@brightcart.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-24T10:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - request

Thanks, Jordan — tagging Ben for commercial and Lauren for ops/rollback acceptance.

High level approach we recommend for short launch windows: an escrowed capacity uplift + prorated true-up. Key bullets:
- Scope: temporary headroom to cover peak up to 4M tokens/day for a configurable window (we typically do 7, 10, or 14 day options).
- Billing: prorated uplift for the window + standard per-token for overrun; we can apply a one-time launch credit to the uplift portion.
- Ops: we’ll schedule a rateband change (API and quota) at the agreed time and set an automatic rollback after the window unless you request an extension.
- Safeguards: rate throttling thresholds and an alert runbook; optionally we can put a hard throttle if you prefer (prevents unexpected over-spend).

I’ve attached a draft of the escrow terms and our recent usage report to give you a starting point. Ben — can you put numbers to a 10-day uplift (prorated pricing + suggested launch credit) and confirm PO/DocuSign requirements? Lauren — can you confirm the earliest time ops can flip the rateband and what rollback automation looks like?

Camila
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com

---
Attachments: escrow_terms.pdf (pdf), usage_report.csv (csv)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@brightcart.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-24T14:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - proposal

Jordan — quick commercial proposal below (we can tighten after Camila/ops confirm window).

Proposal (10-day window, effective 2026-08-31 18:00 PDT → 2026-09-10 23:59 PDT):
- Capacity: uplift to cover up to 4M tokens/day (headroom over baseline).
- Pricing: uplift usage billed at a discounted uplift rate (30% off our standard hosted uplift rate) for the 10-day period. Any overrun beyond the capped uplift will be billed at standard overrun rates.
- Credit: Redwood will apply a one-time launch credit equal to 20% of uplift charges for that window (applied to the first invoice following the event).
- PO & contract: signed amendment or DocuSign required prior to schedule. We can generate an amendment that attaches to the current SOW (Deal DC-9831).

We’ll need a short form PO (purchase order) referencing DC-9831 and a signed amendment; turnaround time for DocuSign once countersigned is typically <24 hours. I’ve included a draft amendment for review.

If that works we’ll start the ops ticket and schedule the rateband change.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com

Date: 2026-08-25T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - proposal

Thanks Ben — that structure looks reasonable and the credit helps. A couple of operational clarifications before we sign:

1) Rollback SLA — if we request automatic rollback at the end of the 10-day window, how quickly will Redwood restore baseline limits? We need <= 15 minutes guaranteed for rollback and a follow-up note when completed.
2) Rateband change API — can you confirm whether this is a documented API call we can use in our CI (i.e., an endpoint to increase/decrease quotas) or whether it requires manual intervention by Redwood ops? Ideally we trigger via API and get an audit log.

If those two items are satisfied we can route PO and countersign the amendment today.

Jordan
Jordan Lee
Launch Ops — BrightCart

Subject: Re: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - proposal / ops plan

Jordan — good questions. Ops confirmation below and next steps to sign.

Rollback SLA
- We will implement an automated rollback at the scheduled window end. Ops will execute the rollback and verify baseline limits are restored; we commit to a 15-minute SLO from the rollback trigger to full restoration and will send an automated confirmation email and a trace id for the change.

Rateband change API
- Ops supports a documented admin API for quota changes (internal use only). For customers we run the change via our control plane and provide a signed JWT callback/audit entry that BrightCart can ingest. We can add a webhook or supply the audit log entry to your S3 bucket for integration with your CI if preferred.

Immediate next steps
1) Ben to generate DocuSign amendment referencing DC-9831 (Ben: please include the 20% launch credit language).
2) BrightCart to send PO referencing DC-9831 and the amendment.
3) Upon countersign, Redwood ops will schedule the rateband change for 2026-08-31 18:00 PDT and create JIRA ticket JIRA-OPS-9821 for runbook/rollback.

I’ve attached the amendment draft and the escrow terms we referenced. If BrightCart can send PO + countersign today we’ll prioritize the ops change and confirm the rollback automation details in JIRA.

Camila

text:
From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@brightcart.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-24T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - request

Camila —

We have a hard launch scheduled for 2026-09-01 and our traffic projections show a concentrated spike during the first 10 days (mainly U.S. traffic). We expect baseline traffic of ~800k tokens/day, rising to a peak of ~3M tokens/day for ~7–10 days during promos.

Two asks:
1) Can Redwood provide a short-term uplift (rate-limit / quota increase) to cover that window? We need the capacity change to be in place by 2026-08-31 18:00 PDT.
2) What would the commercial terms look like for temporary overrun protections (prorated true-up, any launch credits, and rollback guarantees)?

We want to avoid a hard product disruption mid-launch. BrightCart is evaluating a dedicated contract but this is the immediate risk. If you can propose a quick path (pricing + ops steps) by EOD tomorrow that would help our stakeholders sign off.

Jordan
Jordan Lee
Launch Ops — BrightCart
launchops@brightcart.com
(415) 555-0198

---
Attachment: traffic_projection_q3.csv (csv)


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@brightcart.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-24T10:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - request

Thanks, Jordan — tagging Ben for commercial and Lauren for ops/rollback acceptance.

High level approach we recommend for short launch windows: an escrowed capacity uplift + prorated true-up. Key bullets:
- Scope: temporary headroom to cover peak up to 4M tokens/day for a configurable window (we typically do 7, 10, or 14 day options).
- Billing: prorated uplift for the window + standard per-token for overrun; we can apply a one-time launch credit to the uplift portion.
- Ops: we’ll schedule a rateband change (API and quota) at the agreed time and set an automatic rollback after the window unless you request an extension.
- Safeguards: rate throttling thresholds and an alert runbook; optionally we can put a hard throttle if you prefer (prevents unexpected over-spend).

I’ve attached a draft of the escrow terms and our recent usage report to give you a starting point. Ben — can you put numbers to a 10-day uplift (prorated pricing + suggested launch credit) and confirm PO/DocuSign requirements? Lauren — can you confirm the earliest time ops can flip the rateband and what rollback automation looks like?

Camila
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com

---
Attachments: escrow_terms.pdf (pdf), usage_report.csv (csv)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@brightcart.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-24T14:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - proposal

Jordan — quick commercial proposal below (we can tighten after Camila/ops confirm window).

Proposal (10-day window, effective 2026-08-31 18:00 PDT → 2026-09-10 23:59 PDT):
- Capacity: uplift to cover up to 4M tokens/day (headroom over baseline).
- Pricing: uplift usage billed at a discounted uplift rate (30% off our standard hosted uplift rate) for the 10-day period. Any overrun beyond the capped uplift will be billed at standard overrun rates.
- Credit: Redwood will apply a one-time launch credit equal to 20% of uplift charges for that window (applied to the first invoice following the event).
- PO & contract: signed amendment or DocuSign required prior to schedule. We can generate an amendment that attaches to the current SOW (Deal DC-9831).

We’ll need a short form PO (purchase order) referencing DC-9831 and a signed amendment; turnaround time for DocuSign once countersigned is typically <24 hours. I’ve included a draft amendment for review.

If that works we’ll start the ops ticket and schedule the rateband change.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com

---
Attachments: amendment_draft.docx (docx)


From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@brightcart.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-25T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - proposal

Thanks Ben — that structure looks reasonable and the credit helps. A couple of operational clarifications before we sign:

1) Rollback SLA — if we request automatic rollback at the end of the 10-day window, how quickly will Redwood restore baseline limits? We need <= 15 minutes guaranteed for rollback and a follow-up note when completed.
2) Rateband change API — can you confirm whether this is a documented API call we can use in our CI (i.e., an endpoint to increase/decrease quotas) or whether it requires manual intervention by Redwood ops? Ideally we trigger via API and get an audit log.

If those two items are satisfied we can route PO and countersign the amendment today.

Jordan
Jordan Lee
Launch Ops — BrightCart

Subject: Re: Temporary throughput uplift for BrightCart launch - proposal / ops plan

Jordan — good questions. Ops confirmation below and next steps to sign.

Rollback SLA
- We will implement an automated rollback at the scheduled window end. Ops will execute the rollback and verify baseline limits are restored; we commit to a 15-minute SLO from the rollback trigger to full restoration and will send an automated confirmation email and a trace id for the change.

Rateband change API
- Ops supports a documented admin API for quota changes (internal use only). For customers we run the change via our control plane and provide a signed JWT callback/audit entry that BrightCart can ingest. We can add a webhook or supply the audit log entry to your S3 bucket for integration with your CI if preferred.

Immediate next steps
1) Ben to generate DocuSign amendment referencing DC-9831 (Ben: please include the 20% launch credit language).
2) BrightCart to send PO referencing DC-9831 and the amendment.
3) Upon countersign, Redwood ops will schedule the rateband change for 2026-08-31 18:00 PDT and create JIRA ticket JIRA-OPS-9821 for runbook/rollback.

I’ve attached the amendment draft and the escrow terms we referenced. If BrightCart can send PO + countersign today we’ll prioritize the ops change and confirm the rollback automation details in JIRA.

Camila

title: RFI: Dedicated capacity questionnaire — GPU runway & SLA carve-outs
preview:
From: Ava Thompson <ava.thompson@novacorp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity questionnaire — NovaCorp initial RFP

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. As discussed, attached is NovaCorp's dedicated-capacity questionnaire (PDF) — the procurement and SRE teams asked us to capture a few hard constraints so we can evaluate committed capacity options: 

- Target steady-state throughput: ~200,000 tokens/minute across a mix of assistant-style chat (≈60%) and embeddings (≈40%) with typical prompt lengths 50-400 tokens.
- Tail SLA: 99th pct latency target for assistant requests <= 200ms (token generation latency considered).
- Expected burst: up to 3x daily peak sustained for up to 30 minutes during business hours.
- Data controls: VPC private deployment required for model weights + audit logs; SOC2 evidence will be requested.

Questions are inline in the attached PDF, but two quick asks up-front: 1) recommended GPU families and mix for that throughput with reasonable headroom; 2) reservation phasing options (quarterly ramp vs annual commit) — NovaCorp prefers a 3-phase runway model.

We hope to have procurement answers by end of month. Can you confirm the right person on your side to work through sizing and SLA carve-outs?

Thanks,
Ava Thompson
Senior Procurement Manager
NovaCorp

Attachment: NovaCorp_Dedicated_RFP_v2.pdf (application/pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Thompson <ava.thompson@novacorp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Aug 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity questionnaire — NovaCorp initial RFP

Hi Ava —

Appreciate the clear constraints. Karthik and I took a first pass on sizing and I attached a lightweight workbook that maps throughput bands to GPU mixes and estimated per-token unit cost. High level notes: 

- Baseline recommendation: A100 80GB nodes for steady-state throughput (good balance of memory and throughput) + a small H100 pool for low-latency tail requests and caching-heavy workloads.
- Batching & KV caching: We can target the 200k tokens/min with a 10–15% headroom using dynamic continuous batching and prefix/KV caching for assistant sessions — this reduces both cost and tail exposure.
- Burst handling: Recommend a burst bucket (burst credits) that allows up to ~2.5–3x for short periods with graceful fallback to a quantized variant if sustained.

I attached: runway_workbook_v1.xlsx — it includes three phased commitment options (90d ramp, 6mo staged, annual commit) and an example cost curve. Next steps: 1) confirm NovaCorp's expected steady-state vs burst split by month; 2) we can run a short traffic profile test in our hosted environment to validate tail behavior.

Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachment: runway_workbook_v1.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ava Thompson <ava.thompson@novacorp.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Aug 2026 14:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity questionnaire — NovaCorp initial RFP

Ava — quick follow-up from our infra team after reviewing the workbook: 

1) GPU mix rationale: A100 80GB provides predictable streaming throughput for batching-heavy embedding workloads. H100s are a good fit for short-run low-latency interactive sessions (assistant) but are costlier per-hour. A blended pool (≈70% A100 / 30% H100) hits your latency/cost goals in most run-rate scenarios.

2) SLA carve-outs: We can support negotiated maintenance windows and a scheduled monthly maintenance allowance (e.g., up to 4 hours/month) outside of which standard SLA credits apply. For emergency maintenance that impacts availability we provide prioritized fallbacks to hosted compatible model variants.

3) Quantization/variant strategy: To meet strict tail budgets we recommend a canary lane using an int8 quantized variant with automatic model-fallback policy after X ms (configurable). This preserves perceived availability while bounding cost.

Monica — I copied you for pricing/commitment modeling and to flag potential procurement questions around phased billing.

—Karthik
Senior Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

From: Samuel Reed <sam.reed@procure-nova.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Thompson <ava.thompson@novacorp.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity questionnaire — NovaCorp initial RFP

Thanks for the quick analysis — a couple of procurement items before we can move to a formal quote: 

- Can you provide a sample phasing schedule (quarter-by-quarter) showing how usage ramp maps to billing/credits and early-termination exposure?
- We need a draft DocuSign contract + a list of redlines you typically accept for enterprise reserved capacity.
- SOC2/ISO evidence: please confirm the audit artifacts and a contact for a security call next week.

Also: NovaCorp would prefer a 3-phase runway: onboarding (month 0–3), scale (month 4–9), steady state (month 10+). Can you produce cost curves and a short note on slot eviction policies if H100 nodes are preempted?

Samuel Reed
Head of Procurement, NovaCorp

- Phased schedules: (A) rapid 90-day ramp: higher initial commit, steeper discounts; (B) 6-month staged: balanced; (C) annual commit: best unit economics. Each schedule includes clear early-termination exposure and a credit amortization table.
